summary: Customer testimonial about Cavallo SIMPLE Boots used to transition an Ohio horse to barefoot, helping it cross a stone path comfortably and recommended to a farrier and vet.
Announcement that Cavallo doubled the ACTHA Regional High Point Awards prize pot to $50,000 for riders whose horses wear Cavallo hoof boots on the trail.
Product introduction for the new Cavallo lightweight aluminum Sports Chair with attached side table, shoulder strap, and storage pockets for trail riders and campers.
Educational post arguing horse boots are a better alternative to nailed metal horseshoes, explaining the hoof's natural shock-absorbing mechanics and barefoot performance.
Explainer on barefoot horse care comparing the effects of metal shoes versus going barefoot, covering hoof structures like the frog, bars, and sole and farrier trimming.
Customer story about completing the rocky 33-mile Echo Lakes to Barker Pass segment of the Tahoe Rim Trail on a horse wearing Cavallo boots, contrasted with friends' troublesome Easyboots.
Q&A feature inviting readers to submit hoof and barefoot-transition questions to veterinarian Dr. Geertsema, with a bio of his veterinary and farrier background.
primary_cta: Submit a question to Dr. Geertsema

summary: Inaugural Cavallo blog welcome post inviting readers to subscribe for weekly horse stories, featuring Facebook customer comments and a Dream Riders therapeutic-riding donation note.
Account of barefoot-hoof researcher Brian Hampson visiting Cavallo president Carole Herder's ranch, discussing Australian Brumbies, wild horse populations, and hoof science.
Report on the 6th IAVRPT veterinary rehabilitation symposium in Auburn, Alabama, citing physiotherapist Sue Palmer's research and other speakers as backing for Cavallo's approach.
Reflective post on how horses help people as companions and working animals, alongside how Cavallo products (hoof boots, saddle pads, fly masks) help horses.
Feature drawing parallels between Mexico's Raramuri runners and Australia's wild Brumbies, exploring barefoot/minimal-footwear running and what it teaches about natural hoof and foot health.
